heading: Reports of investigations
label: 6305 Reports of investigations
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource section (U.S. Code)630546:239(g)46:239(k)Section 6305 requires the Secretary to prescribe regulations about the form and manner of reports of investigations of marine casualties. It also provides that the reports shall be available to the public, except for any information they contain related to national security.

Amendments1998—Subsec. (b). substituted “. This subsection does not require the release of information described by or protected from disclosure by another law of the United States.” for “, except to the extent that they contain information related to the national security.”Pub. L. 105–383section 552(b) of title 5
amendments: Amendments1998—Subsec. (b). substituted “. This subsection does not require the release of information described by or protected from disclosure by another law of the United States.” for “, except to the extent that they contain information related to the national security.”Pub. L. 105–383section 552(b) of title 5

children.label: a
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe regulations about the form and manner of reports of investigations conducted under this part.

children.label: b
children.text: Reports of investigations conducted under this part shall be made available to the public. This subsection does not require the release of information described by or protected from disclosure by another law of the United States.section 552(b) of title 5
